audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Timothy text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for household care, widows, elders, accusations, impartiality, health note, visible/hidden works.

verses.restored: Do not rebuke an older man sharply, but encourage as father, younger men as siblings,
older women as mothers, younger women as sisters, in all purity.
Honor widows who are truly widows.
But if any widow has children or grandchildren, let them first learn to practice reverence toward their own household and to give repayment to parents, for this is acceptable before the Divine.
Now the one truly a widow and left alone has hoped upon the Divine and remains in petitions and prayers night and day.
But the one living in indulgence has died while living.
Command these things also, so that they may be above reproach.
But if anyone does not provide for one's own, and especially for household members, one has denied the trust and is worse than an untrusting one.
Let a widow be enrolled not less than sixty years old, having been wife of one husband,
being witnessed to in beautiful/good works: if she raised children, showed hospitality, washed feet of holy ones, helped pressured ones, followed every beautiful/good work.
But younger widows refuse/enroll not; for when desires draw them away from Messiah, they want to marry,
having judgment because they set aside the first trust/pledge.
And at the same time they also learn to be idle, going around houses, and not only idle but also gossiping and meddling, speaking things not necessary.
Therefore I desire younger widows to marry, bear children, manage households, give no starting point to the opposing one for reproach.
For already some turned aside after the adversarial one.
If any trusting woman has widows, let her assist them, and let not the assembly be burdened, so that it may assist those truly widows.
Let the elders leading beautifully/well be considered worthy of double honor, especially those laboring in word and teaching.
For the Scripture says, You shall not muzzle an ox threshing, and, The worker is worthy of wages.
Do not receive an accusation against an elder except upon two or three witnesses.
Those sinning/misaligning, expose before all, so that the rest may have reverence.
I solemnly testify before the Divine and Messiah Jesus and the chosen messengers, that you guard these things without prejudgment, doing nothing according to partiality.
Lay hands quickly on no one, nor share in others' sins/misalignments; keep yourself pure.
No longer drink water only, but use a little wine because of your stomach and your frequent weaknesses.
The sins/misalignments of some humans are evident beforehand, going before into judgment, but for others they also follow after.
Likewise also the beautiful/good works are evident beforehand, and those otherwise cannot be hidden.

translation_notes: Widow care is economic and communal; do not reduce it to moral policing. The chapter distinguishes family responsibility and assembly responsibility.
Accusation and discipline rules require witness, impartiality, and caution against hasty authorization.
The wine/stomach verse is a practical health note, not a spirituality test.
